title: UI: server health indicator — show disconnected state when server is unreachable
description:
Goal: The UI must clearly indicate when the server connection is lost so users don't type into a dead interface.

Specs:
- Add a persistent health check (poll /api/health every 5-10 seconds)
- When server is unreachable: show a prominent banner/overlay (e.g. red bar at top: 'Server disconnected — reconnecting...')
- Disable the chat input (or show warning) so users don't lose typed messages
- When reconnected: dismiss banner, restore input, optionally show 'Reconnected' briefly
- Also detect WebSocket disconnection (if using WS for real-time updates)
- Draft/typed text should be preserved in localStorage so it survives a page reload after reconnection

Verification:
- Stop the server (launchctl stop), UI shows disconnected indicator within 10 seconds
- Restart the server, UI shows reconnected state
- Any text typed during disconnection is preserved
- Chat input is visually disabled or warns during disconnection

Safety:
- Do NOT auto-submit queued messages on reconnect — let the user decide
- Health check polling must not flood the server — 5-10 second interval, back off on repeated failures
- Do NOT change any server-side code — frontend only

title: Protect config.json and critical files from worker git operations
description:
Goal: Workers must never wipe config.json or other machine-specific files via git reset/clean/checkout.

Specs:
- Add config.json to .gitignore (it's machine-specific, should never be in git)
- Add a .gitignore rule for any other machine-specific files (e.g. workspace-state.json)
- Worker AGENTS.md instructions must explicitly say: NEVER run git reset --hard on main, NEVER run git clean -fd on the main worktree
- Shepherd dispatch should set up worktrees with config.json symlinked or copied from the main repo
- Consider adding a pre-commit hook or safety check that prevents committing config.json

Verification:
- config.json is in .gitignore
- git reset --hard in a worktree does not affect config.json in the main repo
- Workers on branches cannot corrupt main's working directory

Safety:
- Do NOT delete existing config.json
- If config.json is already tracked in git, untrack it (git rm --cached) but keep the file

close_reason: Done — 47 E2E tests covering dashboard, chat, workspace switching, bead CRUD, modal, scheduler

title: Epic: Comprehensive Test Coverage
description: Epic tracking bead. Sub-beads: versioned API contracts, UI functional tests, unit test gaps, CI hardening, error path tests.

title: Fix message delivery checkmarks: thicker strokes, bottom-right fixed position
description:
Goal: Make the WhatsApp-style delivery checkmarks more visible and properly positioned.

Specs:
- Checkmark strokes are too thin — the green color doesn't pop when they transition from grey to green. Make strokes thicker/bolder.
- Checkmarks should be fixed position in the bottom-right corner of the chat area, not inline with messages.
- Single grey check = sent to server
- Double grey checks = delivered to CLI/relay
- Double green checks = seen/being processed by agent

Verification:
- Checkmarks are clearly visible at a glance
- Green color is distinct from grey state
- Position is fixed bottom-right of chat area

Safety:
- Do NOT change the checkmark state logic — only visual styling and positioning
